project_structure: Cocos Creator 2.x extension project with three main packages: ccc-state-controller (installer extension with panel UI), ccc-state-controller-core (TypeScript game logic), and ccc-smart-component-manager (reference implementation). Test environment is isolated in tests/ directory with separate node_modules. Extension packages follow Cocos Creator 2.x pattern: main.js for IPC handlers, panel/index.js for UI, src/ for business logic.

relevant_files.rationale: Central IPC message hub that coordinates communication between Cocos Editor and panel UI. Contains all message handlers (install, update, uninstall, get-status) that bridge the installer module with the Editor API.

key_code.description: IPC handler for installation, calls installer.install() and sends progress updates via Editor.Ipc.sendToPanel
Critical integration point that refreshes Cocos Editor asset database after file operations

relevant_files.topic_relation: Integration-points exploration identifies this as the main coordinator between Cocos Creator Editor API (Editor.Panel, Editor.Ipc, Editor.Dialog) and the installer business logic

relevant_files.rationale: Panel UI implementation using Editor.Panel.extend() pattern. Contains IPC client code that communicates with main.js via Editor.Ipc.sendToMain. This is the target for ui-ux-pro-max refactoring.

key_code.description: Panel initialization via IPC - receives corePath, targetPath, and coreVersion from main process
Cocos Creator 2.x panel extension pattern with CSS, HTML template, element bindings, and IPC message handlers

relevant_files.topic_relation: Integration-points exploration targets this file for UI refactoring - it uses Editor.Panel.extend pattern which must be preserved while improving UX with ui-ux-pro-max guidelines

relevant_files.rationale: Core installer business logic module that exports install/update/validate/rollback functions. Called by main.js IPC handlers. Must be covered by unit tests.

key_code.description: Main installation function orchestrating versionManager, fileCopier, and validator modules
Returns installation status with version, files list, and validation state

relevant_files.topic_relation: Integration-points exploration identifies this as the business logic layer that connects installer modules (version-manager, file-copier, validator) with the extension IPC layer

relevant_files.rationale: Existing integration test suite for installer module with 5 test suites (fresh install, version update, validation, rollback, edge cases). Provides pattern for unit test structure.

key_code.description: Test context factory creating isolated test directories with mock-core and target folders
Defines files to validate during installation tests - shared constant with validator.js

relevant_files.topic_relation: Integration-points exploration uses this as reference pattern for test structure, helper functions, and mock creation approach

relevant_files.rationale: Test utility module providing mock creation (createMockCore, createMockInstallation), assertions, and file operations. Essential for writing new tests.

key_code.description: Creates mock core package structure matching actual core package for isolated testing
Assertion helper that filters warning messages from errors array when checking success

relevant_files.topic_relation: Integration-points exploration identifies this as the test infrastructure connecting test files with mock file system operations

relevant_files.rationale: Existing unit test for StateController core component using Jest with Cocos Creator mock environment. Demonstrates mocking pattern for cc namespace and Editor global.

key_code.description: Mock environment setup for Cocos Creator cc namespace and Editor global object
Example of testing cache invalidation and rebuild patterns in StateController

relevant_files.topic_relation: Integration-points exploration uses this as reference for Jest test patterns with Cocos Creator mocking in isolated test environment

relevant_files.rationale: Jest configuration for isolated test environment in tests/ directory. Defines moduleNameMapper for assets/* paths and setupFiles for Cocos environment mocking.

key_code.description: Maps assets/* imports to project root assets directory for test resolution
Test environment setup files for Cocos Creator API mocking

relevant_files.topic_relation: Integration-points exploration identifies this as the test configuration connecting Jest runner with Cocos Creator mock environment

relevant_files.rationale: UI/UX design skill with comprehensive guidelines for accessibility, touch interactions, performance, layout, and typography. Will be used to refactor panel UI.

key_code.description: CLI command to generate complete design system with pattern, style, colors, typography
Checklist for visual quality, interaction, light/dark mode, layout, and accessibility before delivery

relevant_files.topic_relation: Integration-points exploration identifies this skill as the design system source for UI refactoring task - provides styles, colors, and UX guidelines

relevant_files.rationale: Reference implementation of panel logic using similar patterns (i18n-helper, data-manager, logger). Shows how another extension handles panel state and Editor integration.

key_code.description: Panel initialization pattern with debounce control, keyboard shortcuts, and theme application

relevant_files.topic_relation: Integration-points exploration uses this as reference for panel patterns including i18n integration, data management, and keyboard handling

patterns: 1. **Extension IPC Pattern**: main.js exports messages object with handlers; panel communicates via Editor.Ipc.sendToMain/sendToPanel. 2. **Panel Extension Pattern**: Editor.Panel.extend({style, template, $, ready, messages}) for Cocos Creator 2.x. 3. **Test Isolation Pattern**: tests/ directory has own node_modules, jest.config maps assets/* to parent directory. 4. **Mock Creation Pattern**: test-helper.js provides createMockCore/createMockInstallation for file system mocking. 5. **Cocos Mock Pattern**: beforeAll sets up global.cc and global.Editor objects with jest.fn() mocks.
dependencies: 1. **External**: Cocos Creator 2.x Editor API (Editor.Panel, Editor.Ipc, Editor.Dialog, Editor.Project). 2. **Internal**: installer module (version-manager, file-copier, validator). 3. **Test**: jest, ts-jest, jest-canvas-mock (isolated in tests/node_modules). 4. **UI**: ui-ux-pro-max skill (Python scripts for design system generation). 5. **Core Package**: ccc-state-controller-core provides TypeScript source files installed to projects.
integration_points: 1. **Editor.Ipc** (main.js L89, L207, L234, L253, L270, L290, L327, L339): Main-to-panel and main-to-all communication for progress updates and asset refresh. 2. **Editor.Panel.open** (main.js L69): Opens installer panel from menu. 3. **Editor.Dialog.showOpenDialog** (main.js L166-L176): Directory selection dialog integration. 4. **Editor.Project.path / Editor.projectInfo.path** (main.js L18-L23): Project path resolution for default installation target. 5. **fs / path** (installer/index.js): Node.js file system operations for copy/validate/uninstall. 6. **Panel-to-Main IPC** (panel/index.js L432, L459, L482, L521, L552, L585, L612): Panel sends requests to main.js handlers. 7. **Test-to-Source Mapping** (jest.config.local.js L27): moduleNameMapper connects tests/assets/* imports to actual source files.
constraints: 1. **Cocos Creator 2.x Compatibility**: Must use Editor.Panel.extend pattern, cannot use modern frameworks. 2. **Extension Runtime**: Panel runs in Electron renderer context with Node.js integration. 3. **Test Isolation**: Tests run in jsdom environment with mocked Cocos API, not real Editor. 4. **IPC Async**: All Editor.Ipc calls are asynchronous with callback pattern. 5. **File System Access**: Main process has full fs access, panel has limited access via IPC. 6. **UI Framework**: Panel uses native Cocos UI elements (ui-input, ui-button) not standard HTML. 7. **Version File Format**: .ccc-state-controller-version stores semantic version string (x.y.z).

clarification_needs.question: Should the panel UI refactoring preserve the existing Editor.Panel.extend pattern or consider alternative approaches?
clarification_needs.context: The current panel uses Cocos Creator 2.x native pattern with inline CSS and template strings. ui-ux-pro-max provides modern design guidelines but the technical constraints require Cocos compatibility.
options: Preserve Editor.Panel.extend pattern, apply ui-ux-pro-max only to CSS styling and layout improvements
Refactor to use a build step (like ccc-smart-component-manager/panel/build.js) to separate logic from template
Minimal changes - only fix accessibility and contrast issues without structural refactoring

clarification_needs.question: What level of unit test coverage is expected for the installer module?
clarification_needs.context: Currently tests/installer.test.js covers integration scenarios. packages/ccc-state-controller-core/tests/ shows Jest unit test patterns. Need to clarify scope for test coverage task.
options: Full coverage: Add unit tests for each installer module (version-manager, file-copier, validator) separately
Integration focus: Expand existing installer.test.js with more edge cases and error scenarios
Critical path only: Focus tests on install/update/validate core workflows

clarification_needs.question: Should test infrastructure be shared between packages/ccc-state-controller and packages/ccc-state-controller-core?
clarification_needs.context: Currently ccc-state-controller has tests/ with own jest setup, ccc-state-controller-core has tests/ with different patterns. Tests also exist in root tests/ for game logic.
options: Consolidate all tests into root tests/ directory with shared configuration
Keep package-specific tests separate but share test utilities (mock helpers, assertions)
Maintain complete separation - each package has independent test setup

_metadata.task_description: 优化 ccc-state-controller 项目：1) 参考 tests/ 目录结构完善单元测试覆盖 2) 使用 ui-ux-pro-max 重构 packages/ccc-state-controller 面板UI
